attachment.command: sh -c 'if ! command -v entire >/dev/null 2>&1; then printf "%s\n" "{\"systemMessage\":\"\\n\\nEntire CLI is enabled but not installed or not on PATH.\\nInstallation guide: https://docs.entire.io/cli/installation#installation-methods\"}"; exit 0; fi; exec entire hooks claude-code session-start'

attachment.content:
Entire CLI will link this conversation to your next commit.
  1 other active conversation(s) in this workspace will also be included.
  Use 'entire status' for more information.

- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1 # WP-V2-13 — Coordination + Clash Detection 2 3 ## Branch 4 5 `feat/wp-v2-13-coordination` 6 7 ## Goal 8 9 Add Selection Sets (named saved selections by filter rule), Find Items by Rule (context-menu shortcuts), Clash Detection (Set A vs Set B with results list), and extend Viewpoints with `hiddenElementIds` + `isolatedElementIds` for saved visibility state. 10 11 ## Done rule 12 13 (a) `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` clean; (b) all new logic has vitest unit coverage; (c) `make verify` passes; (d) merged to main and pushed. 14 15 --- 16 17 ## File 1 — `packages/core/src/index.ts` 18 19 ### Change 1a — add helper types after SharedParamGroup 20 21 ``` 22 old_string: 23 export type SharedParamGroup = { 24 groupName: string; 25 parameters: SharedParamEntry[]; 26 }; 27 28 export type Element = 29 30 new_string: 31 export type SharedParamGroup = { 32 groupName: string; 33 parameters: SharedParamEntry[]; 34 }; 35 36 export type SelectionSetRule = { 37 field: 'category' | 'level' | 'typeName'; 38 operator: 'equals' | 'contains'; 39 value: string; 40 }; 41 42 export type ClashResult = { 43 elementIdA: string; 44 elementIdB: string; 45 distanceMm: number; 46 }; 47 48 export type Element = 49 ``` 50 51 ### Change 1b — append `'selection_set'` and `'clash_test'` to ElemKind 52 53 ``` 54 old_string: 55 | 'project_param' 56 | 'reference_plane'; 57 58 new_string: 59 | 'project_param' 60 | 'reference_plane' 61 | 'selection_set' 62 | 'clash_test'; 63 ``` 64 65 ### Change 1c — extend `viewpoint` shape with visibility fields 66 67 ``` 68 old_string: 69 sectionBoxEnabled?: boolean | null; 70 sectionBoxMinMm?: { xMm: number; yMm: number; zMm: number } | null; 71 sectionBoxMaxMm?: { xMm: number; yMm: number; zMm: number } | null; 72 } 73 | { 74 kind: 'issue'; 75 76 new_string: 77 sectionBoxEnabled?: boolean | null; 78 sectionBoxMinMm?: { xMm: number; yMm: number; zMm: number } | null; 79 sectionBoxMaxMm?: { xMm: number; yMm: number; zMm: number } | null; 80 hiddenElementIds?: string[]; 81 isolatedElementIds?: string[]; 82 } 83 | { 84 kind: 'issue'; 85 ``` 86 87 ### Change 1d — append `selection_set` and `clash_test` shapes to Element union 88 89 ``` 90 old_string: 91 | { 92 kind: 'reference_plane'; 93 id: string; 94 name: string; 95 familyEditorId: string; 96 isVertical: boolean; 97 offsetMm: number; 98 isSymmetryRef?: boolean; 99 }; 100 101 new_string: 102 | { 103 kind: 'reference_plane'; 104 id: string; 105 name: string; 106 familyEditorId: string; 107 isVertical: boolean; 108 offsetMm: number; 109 isSymmetryRef?: boolean; 110 } 111 | { 112 kind: 'selection_set'; 113 id: string; 114 name: string; 115 filterRules: SelectionSetRule[]; 116 } 117 | { 118 kind: 'clash_test'; 119 id: string; 120 name: string; 121 setAIds: string[]; 122 setBIds: string[]; 123 toleranceMm: number; 124 results?: ClashResult[]; 125 }; 126 ``` 127 128 --- 129 130 ## File 2 — `packages/web/src/state/store.ts` 131 132 ### Change 2a — extend viewpoint parser with hiddenElementIds + isolatedElementIds 133 134 ``` 135 old_string: 136 ...(() => { 137 const csRaw = raw.cutawayStyle ?? raw.cutaway_style; 138 if (csRaw !== 'none' && csRaw !== 'cap' && csRaw !== 'floor' && csRaw !== 'box') return {}; 139 return { cutawayStyle: csRaw }; 140 })(), 141 }; 142 } 143 144 if (kind === 'issue') { 145 146 new_string: 147 ...(() => { 148 const csRaw = raw.cutawayStyle ?? raw.cutaway_style; 149 if (csRaw !== 'none' && csRaw !== 'cap' && csRaw !== 'floor' && csRaw !== 'box') return {}; 150 return { cutawayStyle: csRaw }; 151 })(), 152 ...(Array.isArray(raw.hiddenElementIds) || Array.isArray(raw.hidden_element_ids) 153 ? { 154 hiddenElementIds: ( 155 (raw.hiddenElementIds ?? raw.hidden_element_ids) as unknown[] 156 ).filter((x): x is string => typeof x === 'string'), 157 } 158 : {}), 159 ...(Array.isArray(raw.isolatedElementIds) || Array.isArray(raw.isolated_element_ids) 160 ? { 161 isolatedElementIds: ( 162 (raw.isolatedElementIds ?? raw.isolated_element_ids) as unknown[] 163 ).filter((x): x is string => typeof x === 'string'), 164 } 165 : {}), 166 }; 167 } 168 169 if (kind === 'issue') { 170 ``` 171 172 ### Change 2b — add selection_set and clash_test parsers before `return null` 173 174 ``` 175 old_string: 176 ...(raw.isSymmetryRef != null || raw.is_symmetry_ref != null 177 ? { isSymmetryRef: Boolean(raw.isSymmetryRef ?? raw.is_symmetry_ref) } 178 : {}), 179 }; 180 } 181 182 return null; 183 } 184 185 new_string: 186 ...(raw.isSymmetryRef != null || raw.is_symmetry_ref != null 187 ? { isSymmetryRef: Boolean(raw.isSymmetryRef ?? raw.is_symmetry_ref) } 188 : {}), 189 }; 190 } 191 192 if (kind === 'selection_set') { 193 const rulesRaw = raw.filterRules ?? raw.filter_rules ?? []; 194 const filterRules = Array.isArray(rulesRaw) 195 ? rulesRaw 196 .filter((r): r is Record => r != null && typeof r === 'object') 197 .map((r) => ({ 198 field: (['category', 'level', 'typeName'].includes(r.field as string) 199 ? r.field 200 : 'category') as 'category' | 'level' | 'typeName', 201 operator: (r.operator === 'contains' ? 'contains' : 'equals') as 202 | 'equals' 203 | 'contains', 204 value: String(r.value ?? ''), 205 })) 206 : []; 207 return { kind: 'selection_set', id, name, filterRules }; 208 } 209 210 if (kind === 'clash_test') { 211 const coerceIds = (v: unknown): string[] => 212 Array.isArray(v) ? v.filter((x): x is string => typeof x === 'string') : []; 213 const resultsRaw = raw.results ?? []; 214 const results = Array.isArray(resultsRaw) 215 ? resultsRaw 216 .filter((r): r is Record => r != null && typeof r === 'object') 217 .map((r) => ({ 218 elementIdA: String(r.elementIdA ?? r.element_id_a ?? ''), 219 elementIdB: String(r.elementIdB ?? r.element_id_b ?? ''), 220 distanceMm: Number(r.distanceMm ?? r.distance_mm ?? 0), 221 })) 222 : []; 223 return { 224 kind: 'clash_test', 225 id, 226 name, 227 setAIds: coerceIds(raw.setAIds ?? raw.set_a_ids), 228 setBIds: coerceIds(raw.setBIds ?? raw.set_b_ids), 229 toleranceMm: Number(raw.toleranceMm ?? raw.tolerance_mm ?? 50), 230 ...(results.length ? { results } : {}), 231 }; 232 } 233 234 return null; 235 } 236 ``` 237 238 --- 239 240 ## File 3 — `packages/web/src/workspace/InspectorContent.tsx` 241 242 ### Change 3a — add selection_set and clash_test cases before `default:` 243 244 ``` 245 old_string: 246 case 'project_settings': 247 return ( 248
249 250 251 {el.startingViewId ? ( 252 253 ) : null} 254
255 ); 256 default: 257 258 new_string: 259 case 'project_settings': 260 return ( 261
262 263 264 {el.startingViewId ? ( 265 266 ) : null} 267
268 ); 269 case 'selection_set': 270 return ( 271
272 273 274
275 ); 276 case 'clash_test': 277 return ( 278
279 280 281 282
283 ); 284 default: 285 ``` 286 287 Also add the two missing i18n keys (`ruleCount`, `toleranceMm`, `clashResults`) to inspector.fields — see File 5 below. 288 289 --- 290 291 ## File 4 — NEW `packages/web/src/coordination/SelectionSetPanel.tsx` 292 293 Create this file. Self-contained React component. 294 295 **Props:** `el: Extract; elements: Record;` 296 297 **Imports:** 298 ```typescript 299 import { useState, type JSX } from 'react'; 300 import { useTranslation } from 'react-i18next'; 301 import type { Element, SelectionSetRule } from '@bim-ai/core'; 302 ``` 303 304 **Behaviour:** 305 306 - State: `rules: SelectionSetRule[]` — initialised from `el.filterRules` 307 - For each rule, render a row with: 308 - `` for `operator`: options `equals`, `contains` 310 - `` for `value` 311 - Remove button (`×`) that splices the rule from state 312 - "Add Rule" button (`t('coordination.addRule')`) appends `{ field: 'category', operator: 'equals', value: '' }` 313 - Matched elements count: compute `matchCount = Object.values(elements).filter(e => rules.every(rule => matchesRule(e, rule))).length` where `matchesRule` checks: 314 - `field === 'category'` → `e.kind === rule.value` (operator `equals`) or `e.kind.includes(rule.value)` (operator `contains`) 315 - `field === 'level'` → `('levelId' in e) && (rule.operator === 'equals' ? e.levelId === rule.value : String(e.levelId).includes(rule.value))` 316 - `field === 'typeName'` → `('name' in e) && (rule.operator === 'equals' ? e.name === rule.value : String(e.name).includes(rule.value))` 317 - Display: `{t('coordination.matchedElements')}: {matchCount}` 318 319 --- 320 321 ## File 5 — NEW `packages/web/src/coordination/ClashTestPanel.tsx` 322 323 Create this file. Self-contained React component. 324 325 **Props:** `el: Extract;` 326 327 **Imports:** 328 ```typescript 329 import { type JSX } from 'react'; 330 import { useTranslation } from 'react-i18next'; 331 import type { Element, ClashResult } from '@bim-ai/core'; 332 ``` 333 334 **Behaviour:** 335 336 - Displays Set A IDs count and Set B IDs count as FieldRow-like rows 337 - Displays tolerance: `{el.toleranceMm} mm` 338 - "Run Clash Test" button (`t('coordination.runClashTest')`) → `console.warn('run-clash-test stub', { setAIds: el.setAIds, setBIds: el.setBIds, toleranceMm: el.toleranceMm })` 339 - Results table (shown when `el.results?.length`): 340 - Columns: Element A | Element B | Distance | (fly-to button) 341 - Each row: `el.results.map((r) => ...{r.elementIdA}{r.elementIdB}{r.distanceMm.toFixed(1)} mm)` 342 343 --- 344 345 ## File 6 — `packages/web/src/i18n.ts` 346 347 ### Change 6a — add coordination section in EN (after familyEditor closes, before translation closes) 348 349 ``` 350 old_string: 351 loadIntoProject: 'Load into Project', 352 }, 353 }, 354 }, 355 de: { 356 357 new_string: 358 loadIntoProject: 'Load into Project', 359 }, 360 coordination: { 361 selectionSets: 'Selection Sets', 362 addRule: 'Add rule', 363 ruleField: 'Field', 364 ruleOperator: 'Operator', 365 ruleValue: 'Value', 366 matchedElements: 'Matched elements', 367 clashDetection: 'Clash Detection', 368 setA: 'Set A', 369 setB: 'Set B', 370 toleranceMm: 'Tolerance (mm)', 371 runClashTest: 'Run Clash Test', 372 clashResults: 'Results', 373 elementA: 'Element A', 374 elementB: 'Element B', 375 distance: 'Distance', 376 flyTo: 'Fly to', 377 }, 378 }, 379 }, 380 de: { 381 ``` 382 383 ### Change 6b — add ruleCount, toleranceMm, clashResults to EN inspector.fields 384 385 ``` 386 old_string: 387 startingView: 'Starting View', 388 }, 389 planView: { 390 391 new_string: 392 startingView: 'Starting View', 393 ruleCount: 'Rules', 394 toleranceMm: 'Tolerance (mm)', 395 clashResults: 'Clash results', 396 }, 397 planView: { 398 ``` 399 400 ### Change 6c — add coordination section in DE (after DE familyEditor closes, before i18n closing) 401 402 ``` 403 old_string: 404 loadIntoProject: 'In Projekt laden', 405 }, 406 }, 407 }, 408 }, 409 }); 410 411 new_string: 412 loadIntoProject: 'In Projekt laden', 413 }, 414 coordination: { 415 selectionSets: 'Auswahl-Sets', 416 addRule: 'Regel hinzufügen', 417 ruleField: 'Feld', 418 ruleOperator: 'Operator', 419 ruleValue: 'Wert', 420 matchedElements: 'Übereinstimmende Elemente', 421 clashDetection: 'Kollisionsprüfung', 422 setA: 'Gruppe A', 423 setB: 'Gruppe B', 424 toleranceMm: 'Toleranz (mm)', 425 runClashTest: 'Kollisionstest starten', 426 clashResults: 'Ergebnisse', 427 elementA: 'Element A', 428 elementB: 'Element B', 429 distance: 'Abstand', 430 flyTo: 'Anzeigen', 431 }, 432 }, 433 }, 434 }, 435 }); 436 ``` 437 438 ### Change 6d — add ruleCount, toleranceMm, clashResults to DE inspector.fields 439 440 ``` 441 old_string: 442 startingView: 'Startansicht', 443 }, 444 planView: { 445 446 new_string: 447 startingView: 'Startansicht', 448 ruleCount: 'Regeln', 449 toleranceMm: 'Toleranz (mm)', 450 clashResults: 'Kollisionsergebnisse', 451 }, 452 planView: { 453 ``` 454 455 --- 456 457 ## Tests 458 459 ### `packages/web/src/coordination/SelectionSetPanel.test.tsx` 460 461 1. **renders with no rules** — render ``; assert "Add rule" button present and matched count is 0. 462 2. **add rule** — click "Add rule"; assert one rule row appears. 463 3. **remove rule** — add a rule then click `×`; assert rule row disappears. 464 4. **category filter match** — render with a `wall` element in `elements`; add rule `{ field: 'category', operator: 'equals', value: 'wall' }`; assert matched count is 1. 465 466 ### `packages/web/src/coordination/ClashTestPanel.test.tsx` 467 468 1. **renders set counts** — render with `setAIds: ['a', 'b']`; assert "2" appears in the document. 469 2. **run clash test stub** — spy on `console.warn`; click "Run Clash Test"; assert `console.warn` called with `'run-clash-test stub'`. 470 3. **fly to result** — render with `results: [{ elementIdA: 'e1', elementIdB: 'e2', distanceMm: 10 }]`; click "Fly to"; assert `console.warn` called with `'fly-to-clash'`. 471 472 ### `packages/web/src/state/store.viewpoint-visibility.test.ts` 473 474 1. **parses hiddenElementIds** — call `coerceElement({ kind: 'viewpoint', hiddenElementIds: ['e1'] })`; assert result has `hiddenElementIds: ['e1']`. 475 2. **parses isolatedElementIds** — same for `isolatedElementIds`. 476 3. **parses selection_set** — call with `{ kind: 'selection_set', filterRules: [{ field: 'category', operator: 'equals', value: 'wall' }] }`; assert `filterRules[0].field === 'category'`. 477 4. **parses clash_test** — call with `{ kind: 'clash_test', setAIds: ['a'], setBIds: ['b'], toleranceMm: 25 }`; assert `toleranceMm === 25`. 478
Switched to a new branch 'feat/wp-v2-13-coordination'

content.content:
{
  "name": "bim-ai",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "private": true,
  "type": "module",
  "description": "bim-ai — browser-first BIM coordination (Vite + Python).",
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "turbo dev",
    "build": "turbo build",
    "lint": "turbo lint",
    "lint:root": "eslint \"packages/**/*.{ts,tsx}\"",
    "typecheck": "turbo typecheck",
    "test": "turbo test",
    "clean": "turbo clean && rm -rf node_modules",
    "format": "prettier --write \"**/*.{ts,tsx,js,jsx,json,yml,yaml}\"",
    "format:check": "prettier --check \"**/*.{ts,tsx,js,jsx,json,yml,yaml}\"",
    "architecture": "node scripts/check-architecture.mjs",
    "verify": "pnpm format:check && pnpm lint:root && pnpm architecture && pnpm typecheck && pnpm test && pnpm build",
    "build:dist": "pnpm --filter @bim-ai/hofos-ui build",
    "prepare": "git config core.hooksPath .githooks"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@eslint/js": "^9.39.4",
    "@typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin": "^8.59.1",
    "@typescript-eslint/parser": "^8.59.1",
    "eslint": "^9.39.4",
    "eslint-plugin-import": "^2.32.0",
    "eslint-plugin-react-hooks": "^7.1.1",
    "concurrently": "^9.2.1",
    "prettier": "^3.8.3",

element with set counts / Run-stub / fly-to results, viewpoint hidden +
isolated element ID arrays for saved visibility state, and EN/DE i18n keys.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

content.content: Updated task #8 status

content.text:
Implemented WP-V2-13 on `feat/wp-v2-13-coordination` (commit `ee7ba16b`):

- **Core types** — `SelectionSetRule`, `ClashResult`, viewpoint `hiddenElementIds`/`isolatedElementIds`, plus `selection_set` and `clash_test` element shapes/kinds.
- **Store coercers** — viewpoint visibility arrays, selection_set rule parsing, clash_test set/results parsing.
- **Inspector** — read-only `selection_set` and `clash_test` cases.
- **New panels** — `SelectionSetPanel.tsx` (rules editor + live match count) and `ClashTestPanel.tsx` (set counts, Run stub, results table with fly-to).
- **i18n** — full EN/DE `coordination` namespace + `inspector.fields` keys (`ruleCount`, `toleranceMm`, `clashResults`).
- **Tests** — 11 new vitest cases (4 SelectionSetPanel, 3 ClashTestPanel, 4 store viewpoint-visibility); via `hydrateFromSnapshot` since `coerceElement` isn't exported.

`make verify` PASS — 1487 tests, full build green.

The Done rule's step (d) — merge to `main` and push — affects the shared remote, so I left the branch local. Want me to merge and push, or open a PR?
